title: Power of Four
description: Given an integer n, determine if it is a power of four. An integer n is a power of four if there exists an integer x such that n == 4^x (i.e., n can be expressed as 4 raised to the power of some integer).

examples.explanation: 16 can be expressed as 4^2 (4 * 4), thus it is a power of four.
5 cannot be expressed as 4 raised to any integer power, therefore it is not a power of four.
64 can be expressed as 4^3 (4 * 4 * 4), confirming that it is indeed a power of four.

constraints: n must be greater than or equal to 0.
hints: Consider how powers of four are structured in binary representation.
You might find it helpful to use logarithmic properties to solve this problem.
Think about the relationship between powers of two and powers of four.
Check if n is greater than zero before proceeding with further checks.
